rounds.prompt: How many times more books exist today than when Gutenberg printed the first Bible?
rounds.fact: Europe had ~30,000 hand-copied books in 1455; today ~170 million distinct titles exist — 5,600× more, thanks to the printing press Gutenberg invented.

rounds.prompt: How many times taller is the tallest wave ever surfed than a standard swimming pool depth?
rounds.fact: An Olympic pool is 2 m deep; the tallest wave ever surfed was 26.2 m (Sebastian Steudtner, Nazaré, 2020) — 13× deeper than your pool.

rounds.prompt: How many times heavier are Earth's oceans than its entire atmosphere?
rounds.fact: Earth's atmosphere weighs ~5.15 × 10¹⁸ kg; its oceans weigh ~1.4 × 10²¹ kg — the oceans are 270× heavier than all the air above you.

rounds.prompt: How many times farther away is the Andromeda Galaxy than the diameter of the Milky Way?
rounds.fact: The Milky Way is ~100,000 light-years across; Andromeda is ~2.537 million light-years away — 25× our galaxy's width. Yet it's our nearest large neighbour.

rounds.prompt: How many times heavier is Earth than the Great Wall of China?

itemA.name: Great Wall of China (~374 billion kg)

rounds.fact: The Great Wall weighs ~374 billion kg; Earth weighs ~5.97 × 10²⁴ kg — 16 trillion× heavier. The Great Wall is quite literally a rounding error on a planetary scale.
